review_text: Unacceptable for accessibility for wheelchairs ♿️.
We really loved this place. Affordable and a great location.
Great place for kids and adults alike. Caravan sites have good access whilst providing reasonable space between adjacent sites. Well managed with extremely help management and staff.
A great stopover just off the Pacific highway.
Room was good although shower was not too hot. Staff were straight on site to fix the issue, very accommodating. Great location, plenty to do with kids. Out of the hustle and bussel of town

Great spot,

Need to manage the speed that cars travel jumping pillow to boat ramp, as it is unsafe for pedestrians in that particular areA
Great stay. Park was immaculate, heaps for the kids to do and the staff were wonderful. Best caravan park I have been to by far!

I’ve been thinking of a fun and memorable place to take the kids these school holidays, and this holiday park had everything we were looking for. Thank you, Discovery Parks Forster, for creating such a special experience for families—especially the kids 💛

Friendly staff, Very Clean and well maintained holiday park and stunning river view❤️
It was good but to many kids and not a lot of entertainment

We booked the standard 2 bedroom cabin and were lucky enough to receive a complimentary upgrade to the superior river view cabin 105 when we checked in. The river view from the cabin is absolutely stunning, especially while having a BBQ on the verandah. The cabin is very new, clean, and has enough kitchen utensils for simple cooking. It’s such a peaceful, relaxing place, full of kids’ activities during school holidays. The kids had a fantastic time at the water splash park, heated swimming pool, jumping pillow, and enjoyed a lovely sunset walk around the river. Outstanding service from Sammy !

Thank you Discovery Park Forster! We will definitely be returning in the future and highly recommend it to all families with young children.
Quiet on the river very pleasent

Took a gamble booking for a trip from Qld to Vic. Not knowing the area, we were hoping to book somewhere that was close/walk to a shop. Unfortunately it was a 10 to 15 min drive in to town but we still booked it as the park looked good on the internet. Facilities were good. Were meant to stay for 2 nights however, left at 4pm on the second night. Kids were excited to hit the road and there was no point sleeping in the van there just to wake early and go. Kids were keen to keep swimming but people smoking in pool area put us off.

That was the main and almost only negative. People smoking in the pool area around our kids that afternoon. Bad enough we decided to leave.

When we checked out, staff noticed it was a day early and asked if everything was OK. I said sort of. Happy with the park except smoking in the pool area with children. The staff member didn't show any concern and said there weren't really any rules around smoking in pool area. We had noticed there were no signs saying you can't smoke. I am guessing the staff member was a smoker by her lack of reaction or concern but i may be wrong. I would have thought it was a law of some kind  but haven't looked in to it.

Overall great park but I would never stay there again, purely based on they allow smoking in the pool area. It stinks. It's unhealthy, and my kids want to enjoy the pool and fresh air, not cigaret smoke.
We will be camping there in Christmas 2026, booking staff especially Miss Min is awesome. Friendly and helpful people. Cheers. Look forward
A bit out of town but right on the lake and close to sea.  Brand new facilities.  Wide sites with new power and water hook ups.   Great place to stay.

Would give six stars....
Brilliant park..
One of the best parks stayed...
Has everything a family would need..
Pets well catered for .. big off lead park toys provided..
Swimming pool an river sandy beach.
Fishing and play areas for kids..
Highly recommend...
Will go back...

5 Stars no problem. Not because it is 5 star accommodation but because our cabin was exactly as advertised. We had cabin 116. Probably the cheapest and most basic of the pet friendly options. Two bedrooms, 1 queen bed, 4 bunks. The cabin was clean, the shower was hot with plenty of pressure and the bed was comfortable. That's all I need.
This is very basic accommodation. However it was clean and comfortable, the kitchen was functional with enough cutlery and equipment to cook basic meals.
Be aware the long side of the cabin faces west and the whole cabin can be quite hot and only the lounge/kitchen/ dining area is air-conditioned. Other than that, perfectly good accommodation for a couple of nights.

The cabins are nice and clean. The staff are great but I am only giving 3 stars because the 2 nights we stayed we were woken abruptly by workmen driving trucks, using grinders that are very loud at 7 a.m. sharp. I know that is legal time but geez couldn't they spare you until at least 8? So my advice would be to call before booking online and ask if they are doing work. What a shame.

We had one of the best camping weekends here, cabins are spacious and clean. We loved how dog friendly the campground is, fenced off leash park was just amazing! Kids loved the heated pool and water slides. We definitely will be returning back!
Great place for kids

Oh Wow 🤩
How lovely is Discovery Park Forster !
Arrived to a gorgeous orchid and a bottle of champagne 🍾.. thank you so very much Georgie and Dave “discovery park. Forster” and of course my beautiful Daughter Grace (discovery park emerald beach) for this wonderful gift 🎁 xxx
G & A
Stayed 4 nites caravan site was great except tv reception did not happen morning noon and night we were parked other side of pillow jump trampoline.

Great for kids! Some of the facilities was still getting renovated when we were there, the new cabins are cleaned and well equip.
Lots of activities for the kids during the school holidays, such a great place. Will definitely return.

Magical fun for all ages.
Check out the free included water park !

This place is all about the amazing view.

2nd time I stayed here.

First time in a cabin near the pool. Cabin was new and in very good condition.

This time I decided to book a cabin lake front. Cabin is very old and in poor condition . Cabin was clean but had a musty smell.
Self contained but kitchen and kitchenware needs updating. Staying here is all about the amazing view of the lake. Sitting out on the porch and just watching the sunset. Spectacular.

Staff were amazing  park is clean and well organised.

Would recommend but I would stay in the newer cabins and walk down to the lake.

My husband kept his promise with booking a family holiday, he booked the perfect spot for our kids. Family friendly, staff were welcoming on arrival. The splash park and pool are fantastic for the little ones and not small. But what i love the most is the people that surround you friendly vibes,  welcoming, families.

Had loads of fun with leaving the pool for a call to play with slime. The happiness with the kids and the adults was just what was needed for more fun.

Cabin was clean we asked for extra towels with no fuss and a delightful Penny.
Deluxe cabin had air conditioning in all the rooms clean and tidy.

Park is well maintained and clean.

Cafe also onsite for a morning coffee, bacon and egg roll or pizza in the evening

The view along the lake pitchersque with views of cows.
To the Owners you are running an amazing park and to the staff thank you for all your hard work.
very good experience stay. I live in the new cabin everything is clean and the bed comforts

Unfortunately, I was very disappointed with how this holiday park handled an extremely distressing situation.

We had to cancel our booking after my mother was unexpectedly admitted to ICU and placed on life support. I contacted the park immediately, explaining the situation and asking for leniency or even a credit to rebook at a later date. I also offered to provide proof of her ICU admission.

I initially received no response and had to follow up. When they eventually replied, they advised they would still charge the full cancellation fee and only offer a partial credit. There was no empathy or consideration given to the circumstances at all.

What’s particularly frustrating is that had we booked through a third-party platform like Booking.com, we would have been eligible for free cancellation — instead, we were penalised for booking directly.

I understand policies exist, but compassion and basic human understanding matter too. I would strongly recommend thinking twice before booking directly, especially if you expect flexibility or empathy during genuine emergencies.

The discovery park looks to have recently undergone a major renovation with lots of new amenities and attractions. The splash pad and swimming pool are a must visit and if you have kids the jumping pillow, playground and pump track are all onsite. The park features it's own cafe which opens for breakfast and lite lunches. Backing on to the river you can bring your own boat or hire a pedal boat and kayak of this is your thing.

Accommodation - delux cabin sleeps 4 - really clean and tidy and will layed out. Air conditioning in main area and master bedroom which features a king bed. The second bedroom features 2 kind singles and all beds are very comfortable giving you a good night sleep. This cabin is overlooking the lagoon and is very close to the surrounding bush. Featuring beautiful views it does come with a few surprises. If your not into nature then the daily spiders, bush rats and lizards might not be for you. These will come up on your verandah and the lovely huntsman spiders can be found in some not so nice areas.

Splash pad - is simply awesome and you can spend hours here. It's a little cold at first but once wet your good to go. It was not working one of the mornings and looked to be an issue with the pump but was back up and running couple hours later. If your going to watch the kids and not participate there is little to no seating they may want to address this and no shade over any of the splash pad.

The pool - nice large shallow end for younger and little kids which is mostly under cover from a shade cloth. A number of pool chairs for sun baking or watching the kids. The pool is very warm and is set at 31 degrees. On a warm day it feels too hot and the coolness of the splash pad will cool you down.

Jumping pillow - this is located right up at the entry to the park near the powered camp sites. It features a sandy surround so if you fall it will soften the blow. However, no shade cloth means when the sun is out you can't use it as you simply burn due to the heat coming off it.

Camp kitchens - well laid out and with large screen TVs. The also have multiple fridges, dishwashers and air conditioning.

BBQs - they are scattered throughout the park but we encountered several problems.  On 2 occasions we were cooking our food when the bbq decided to lose heat and not want to reignite. This left the food half cooked and luckily we had access to our cabin to finish cooking. The bbq next to it wouldn't ignite at all. After having management look at them they said they were fixed only to have same problems next day. Other people also complaining about the same issues.

Cafe - great for a bite to eat or cold drink. Sit in our outside mostly under cover. Prices are same as any other cafe. You may encounter a 30 to 40 minute wait when they have a few orders.

Park speed limit - be aware the speed limit in the park is 5km. We were out walking our baby and 2 toddlers early morning and a cafe staff member was driving easily 50km and nearly hit us as she was going too fast and in a hurry. She then proceeded to turn and reverse park very quickly like she was late to work. Management not worried about this when informed apparently.

Power to our cabin randomly went out and we had to wait for someone to come and put it back on. Not sure what the issue was but was fixed.

We stopped here for a night, but we ended up staying the whole weekend.
Very nice park good for the families sometimes they have activities

We loved our stay here. It was my husband, our dog and myself.
I love the fact that they allow dogs inside the cabins.
The private enclosed dog park is great.

This is the first time I have ever been to one of these family friendly parks - I’m pleasantly surprised ! This place is a kids dream. The cabin was clean, spacious, and was equipped with a kitchen that had the cookware we needed. The cabin was right next to the water splash park and pool. A cafe was just around the corner. We hired the pedal cart for 30mins. My son loved the jumping pillow. The kids had a blast here. Parking was easy (we drive an EV), the cabin had an electrical port outside to charge the car. Staff were exceptional.
Outstanding as always, every year keeps getting better. Have been coming every year for 30+ years

Very suprised at what we came across at the end of the road, off the main access into Tuncurry & Forster. Only a 10 minute drive into the beaches & shops of Forster/Tuncurry but a lovely, spacious holiday park situated right on the banks of the water sport  loving Wallamba River. Friendly service, immaculate cabins and so much to do. We were very lucky to be there in holidays where the kids were thoroughly entertained with loads of free activities.

Amazing
We will be back

My family and I arrived at this park, researching where we would like to spend the holidays. 1st receptionist was so helpful, showed us where to go on the map, discussed the facilities and let us know availably dates. However there was a 2nd receptionist who was so rude, obviously in need of a day off, condescending and so clearly did not want to speak to us or help us in any way. Our experience with the receptionist actually turned us off exploring this park all together and we ventured elsewhere in Tuncurry. Probably dont want this receptionist as the first impression for this caravan park.

Stayed here while visiting friends in Forster. Perfect 1 bedroom cabin with lovely views of the river. We were next to the Cafe which came in handy for a morning coffee with lovely views. Had a great time.

Great place for family reunions.  Boat ramp very close, noisy during the day.  Cabins are clean and maintained.  Dog friendly facilities including a dog wash.  Linen, utensils etc supplied.  So good, we have booked for 2026 Christmas.  Santa made a visit
Very nice stay overall good experience. Kids enjoyed. Pool was clean and water fun was good.
I asked to clean the room before coming! But unfortunately I find this!
Great location , a shade over the water park would be good , and some more seating in and around the park , we had a great holiday and would recommend to other families

The grey curly hair lady at front desk has an attitude problem. I disregard a mouldy aircon, skid marks on toilet upon entry, broken shower head. Whereas rude people? I just can't stand it. You spoil your visitors whole day due to your stinky attitude. The guy with glasses is an absolute asset to your business. The lady really spoiled it for me. I can't stand her attitude and lack of customer service skills. A simple hello and thank you is all it take. Not a snobby remark for accidentally not returning the bike key that you only need at night time to lock up anyway. Thanks lady!
Loved it here. Will definitely bring the family back soon.
Never got the site we booked as it was vacant when we booked and got moved down the back
Beautiful spot just be prepared that you cant get food delivered so get some food before you crack a can

Beautiful park, great location right on the river, lovely for walks or fishing. Very family friendly, great staff. Short drive to town, so nice and quiet. The winner for us is the water park and pool area, absolutely fantastic! Honestly the best spot for a family holiday. Peddle carts were really fun and the cafe is a great touch! Beautiful new cabins, very clean and spacious. Can't wait to come back!
Clean, nice stopover good price

We just had the best stay at Discovery Parks Forster. Staying right on the river in a beautiful self contained unit that had absolutely everything you could need. The pool area is perfect, warm and plenty of seating. We only stayed 2 nights but cannot wait to come back ASAP

Great holiday park! A short drive from Forster right on the river, lots of new cabins, big new pool and the kids favourite was the splash park with 3 waterslides! Staff were lovely and had activities for the kids to do like pedal karts, scavenger hunt and finding a hidden dolphin each day during the school holiday season.
Amazing staff . Really their management and make the difference there.
Very rude and unhelpful.

A great family park with lots to do for the kids.  Bird life a plenty for the avid watcher.  Amenities were clean, the camp kitchen is very good.  The staff make you feel very welcome.  This was our first time there, and we will be back.  We had an ensuite site near the camp kitchen.

This place is the worst for anyone with kids yea the pool area and everything is all good during the day but of a night it’s a nightmare ice cream truck comes through blaring its music at 9/930 at night they have live music some nights that play until 1 am in the morning and they let people blare music and get drunk all night good luck getting any sleep here

What a great weekend away! I was upgraded on arrival to a superior cabin, which was lovely! Beautiful, clean and comfortable 2 bedroom cabin with a full kitchen, nice quality furnishings. Very pet friendly, my dog loved her holiday, walks along the river, a dog park to run in and only a 15 minute drive to swim at the beach. Theres tourist information, a cafe with a wood fire pizza night each week and so much for kids to do. The staff are all so helpful and friendly and I am already planning another holiday. will definitely be back for another break.

They are currently doing major renovations which I'm sure will be great once done, we went just after weeks of rain so it was abit wet in places. Upon entering we found our caravan site and were initially disappointed with its location. However as we got used to it we enjoyed it, fires allowed in approved fire pits. Toilets and showers were clean, the heated pool and water park were great. We shared a fence line with the permanent residents behind us and we had a constant cigarette smell which was off putting but it is what it is. We would return.

As I found it . Very clean and well maintained  very peaceful and quiet very friendly  staff we were travelling  with two dogs (bull terrier s)  a short walk took a long time as they  like to meet  anyone that would stop and give them a pat .Breakfast  at the shop coffee  and egg and bacon roll every enjoyable I would  highly recommend the park if you want to sit back and enjoy the piece and quiet  and do a day trip now and again  (cabin 26)

Since it is a 20 minute drive to Forster town, it's  location title is a little bit misleading.
Notwithstanding,  we had a nice site by the river. It is a huge Park with hundreds of cabins so I expect it would be very busy in season.

We chose the Discovery Park in Forster as it looked as though we would overlook the water. If you are staying in a cabin there are some wonderful spots but van travellers are relegated to a much less attractive area.  We were originally staying two nights which would have been plenty but the rains came and it was better to stay put. At least we had a slabbed site.  Camp kitchen is located near the cabins which was more than 5 minutes away.  Dump site is also near the cabins so it is obvious that is where the money is now.  They need to rebuild the infrastructure for the new van areas. We did find another dump point across the road which was much closer so look for it. The on site cafe is limited. We went to have lunch on Friday but they had already turned the grill off for cleaning!  At least the amenities block was new and our site was mostly peaceful till families arrived on the weekend.  We were near the jumping pillow that still had adults jumping after dark. Lovely to have a fire. Finally this park is located in Darawank not Forster which is a ten minute drive south.  We made the best of our time. Found the walk through the cabins and onto the river good to get some steps in and it was fabulous value - stay four pay 3. Around $23 a night for power and water! There is an ensuite site near the cabins. Number 25.  And the pool and water areas look like great fun.

The park is big and we were stuck in a new area on the out skirts of the park. Very unattractive. No TV signal at all. There were a lot of permanent vans. There was a dead cow in the river not far from cabins etc. Absolutely horrific!!

We just needed a powered ensuite site at the last minute on our trip home and these guys were great! Admittedly the park was empty as it was a Monday night in winter, and would have been packed solid during summer holidays, but our experience was peaceful, convenient, and cheap!
Would definitely recommend.

It was our first and last visit to that place. The cabin was all clean and check in was with a nice smile, but what happened next - was a total disaster I wish nobody experienced. Once we checked in and settled - power was gone and it was never back till we left. No warnings, no back up generators, no candles, no refund. And there we sat with kids in total darkness tired after a long drive to get there and did’t want to drive again in the stormy weather at night. This place must be closed during storms!

The caravan park was still under renovation
But still it was good great new clean ammenities
The caravan park itself is a little way from the pool, river and cafe but once you walk there it’s all just beautiful
很棒的體驗，房間很大👍🏻
Nice, peaceful, friendly staff and nice accommodation. Thanks for the upgrade.
Had an enjoyable stay with a powered site. Showers and Laundry are very clean. Friendly staff and highly recommend.
Good. However, required to improve the cleanliness Cockroach and ant were in the 2nd bed room.

My wife and I (and fur friend) had a 3 night stay in the cabin next to the boat ramp. What an absolutely beautiful view we had from the large deck overlooking the water. Although the kids pool and play area were nearby we had such a relaxing stay with town only 12 minutes down the road. There’s heaps for the kids to do such as fishing, biking and the water playground looked fantastic. The café nearby satisfied my need for coffee each day and there was a good menu to choose from for meals all day.
We’ll be recommending this holiday park to all our family and friends.

Hi Dave and team,

We just wanted to let you know how much we enjoyed our stay with you.
We really needed a break away and we could not have chosen a better place.
Cabin 15 is a perfect place to relax and unwind,  the river view is amazing. Sitting on the deck watching the world go by, enjoying the local wildlife including a small pod of dolphins playing in the river.
The team here are very welcoming and helpful.
Just a short drive to Forster/Tuncurry for shopping and the on-site Cafe has fabulous coffee and big breakfasts. Who could ask for anything more,  we'll definitely be coming back.
Again thank you, thank you, thank you 😊

Such a beautiful location right by the water! The cabin was lovely, peaceful, and pet-friendly—perfect for travelling with my furbaby. The after-hours check-in and check-out system was super easy, and when I had to call the next day, the customer service was exceptional. Would absolutely stay here again!

We loved our 3 night stay in a cabin in this caravan park. We arrived from Bellingen and before that the Gold Coast, where we copped a lot of rain while camping. It was great to be able to dry our gear out on the cabin's verandah and get some washing done.  Great location on the river that by boat brings your right in to Forster. It is a wonderful park for families as well, but when we were staying there the playground and BMX track were very wet due to the consistent rains before we arrived. There's a great little cafe on site with good and well priced food. The staff were very helpful and friendly. We can recommend this place.
Caravan sites were great. Amenities new and kept clean. Lovely pool, although a few more chairs around the pool would be helpful.

Just had to share my recent experience at Discovery Parks Forster!

What started as a practical pit stop on our long journey from Byron Bay to Sydney with my little 3-year-old sidekick turned into such a delightful surprise.

Enter Dave - the accommodation superhero we didn't know we needed! He answered my call straight away with such cheerfulness that I was immediately put at ease. When I mentioned we'd be arriving at 5:08pm (yep, that specific!), just after their 5pm closing, Dave didn't hesitate to say he'd wait for us. Anyone travelling with a toddler knows how precious these small kindnesses can be!

Upon arrival, I mentioned we were mainly there for the waterpark experience, and Dave went above and beyond by upgrading us to a cabin closer to the waterpark at no extra cost. Talk about understanding the logistics of travelling with a little one before dark!

The cabin itself? Absolutely gorgeous with stunning lake views and just a hop, skip and jump from the waterpark. Perfect for our short stay!

Dave's thoughtfulness continued with a spot-on local pub recommendation and offering a super convenient checkout option since we needed to hit the road at the crack of dawn (4am to be exact).

While our whirlwind visit meant we couldn't fully explore everything the park had to offer, what we did see was clean and well-maintained. But honestly, it was Dave's exceptional hospitality that made this brief stopover memorable.
Would I recommend Discovery Parks Forster? In a heartbeat!

Sometimes it's not just about the facilities (though they were great) but the people who make you feel genuinely welcome. Thanks for the outstanding service, Dave!
Great facilities. Friendly staff. Kids had a great time!

Lakeview cabin 10/10
Waterpark and pool 10/10
Staff friendliness 10/10
Cafe on-site 10/10
Great facilities, very clean and convenient. The staff was friendly and helpful. Kid enjoyed the pool and scooter circuit.

Fantastic caravan park, one of the very best I've stayed at. Highlights were the free paddleboard hire, onsite cafe, splash pad, pool (though it needs more shade), BBQs and jumping pillow. We'll be back.
Got abused by a staff member while on the street outside trying to find reception.  We had a booking but did not stay.

We chose the Forster G'Day Park located on Wollamba River at Darawank which is before you get to Tuncury.  I can see why they called themselves Forster Discovery Park and not Darawank Discovery Park lol.  Reception is located right at the end of the road in opposite the Cafe.

The price here for a caravan site is very good per night whereas if we had have stayed in Tuncurry or Forster, we'd be paying between $50 and $60 per night.  It's only a 20 minute drive into Forster and a pleasant drive too so we didn't mind.

The pool was amazing, there is also a water park & peddle carts for the kids, and they have a cafe as well.  As you can see, the river is beautiful at sunset.

Not a nice experience- We were placed in the "new area'. We were woken up every day with construction noise. Solar panel installation on one side and building works on the other. We were surrounded by dilapidated buildings and construction materials. Not pleasant at all. We have been to this park multiple times but this time it was a terrible experience.

Great place, lovely pool. The Cafe has amazing food. Staff were hostile with eachother in the Cafe. But the food was great.
The location is so nice. My kids loved this place.

They should have a sink at the BBQ area so it's easier to clean up afterwards.
Absolutely love this place the kids are having a blast!!!. The Cabins are clean and the rooms are nicely furnished. The kids are loving the splash park.
Het park is aan modernisering toe. De vloer van ons huisje is heel vies, incl de badkamer vloer. De verlichting geeft geen enkele sfeer, het is helder wit en is fel.

Always enjoyed my annual stays at Discovery parks Forster for 25 years. Never get bored of the river views and sunsets from our cabin balcony. Seen the upgrades for kids such as the splash park, pool, ninja course, pump track and jumping pillow, restaurant and cabins. Perfect position for boats to cruise down the river to Forster too. Nice that this place is dog friendly.
Staff here are always friendly and shout out to David who manages this park very well, is very accommodating to clients and works very hard to make sure the park and cabins are clean and tidy which is always the case.
Thank you David and staff for the wonderful experience!
One of the best camping sites. We would like to thank all the staff for their kindness and friendliness.
What a wonderful park! Love it here and will return

Really enjoyed our stay. Perfect for kids with lots of activities available in the park itself, or a quick drive into the town for even more to do. Rooms were nice and clean, and the staff were all very accommodating. Would definitely come back.

Nothing special but you pay for what you get stayed four nights powered site, if it's cheap it's worth it otherwise you'll find better value elsewhere closer to forster. It's in the middle of nowhere basically 15 min drive to shopping, it's got a river running through it, that's the only thing going for it. Not sure about creepy staff members decent ppl not sure what service is meant to mean like they didn't come & wipe my arse for me or anything. Not sure what some ppl are looking for? Its a holiday park not a 5 star resort. Ive read things like there was bindies in the grass? So wear some thongs or the showers where cold, so shower in the morning not at night. Like i stated at the top of this post, you pay for what you get & anything over $50 for a powered site anytime of year is outrageous enough let alone peak season when u just sit back & laugh at what some places think there worth.
Absolutely perfect family holiday park! Right on the river. Huge pool and water park. Absolute gem
Great location  quite a big park good amenities  staff great Cafe on site what more do you want 😀

Pros: Big pool, splash park, pump track and playground. Cabins overlooking the lake were peaceful. ||Cons: Cleanliness - we stayed for almost a week and didn’t see anyone cleaning the common areas once. There was litter on the grass in the pool area including bottle caps. The cabins had mould in the showers and overall had a dirty feel. ||Would not stay here again.
The cabin was immaculate and very comfortable. Pleasant , helpful staff.
It's not a bad place. Our unit 118 needs a little bit of TLC, but we will go back there. The takeaway joint in the park has extremely good chips, pizzas are pretty good, and service could be better.

This place is truly incredible. Far exceeds expectations!! David from reception was a highlight being super friendly but not over the top, breakfast at the onsite cafe was DEVINE slightly pricey but very well worth the money! The cabin was so well cleaned and presented very well with an added lovely touch of the air conditioner being on when we got there on a very hot afternoon. We stayed right on the lake side meaning we had an incredible view from our balcony and prime position for both the kids to go in the lake and/or pool and splash park. The beds were very comfortable. The ONLY thing I would say that could be an improvement to this IMPECCABLE family resort would be a little more shade around pool and grassed area near splash pad. Thank you very much for our lovely stay and we will 100% be back!!
I arrived early and was put into an upgrade  room very much appreciated. Sammy In reception is most friendly and extremly welcoming. Great job Sammy.
Best place to have relaxing vacation, totally worth. Reasonable price and very accommodating. Staff were so friendly and welcoming.

nice scenery but abit far from town, pool is really cold, the whole week we were there no one was spending more then 2 mintues in the pool before getting out shaking and freezing including all the kids. wet boggy grounds and really confusing when you come into the site not enough signage

Great park, service and easy location.

I need to update my review, I was very disappointed on our last stay, I booked a standard room as I have done in the past, the cabin on our last stay, was extremely tired and had a mouldy odour.

Nice and peaceful, on the river good for boating etc. Clean cabins with all u need for your stay. Nice staff and shop if want a coffee or something to eat. Good rates for such a nice place.Currently upgrading park with more cabins and new pool etc.

Had a wonderful family vacation here!
Reasonable prices and very accomodating when we decided to upgrade our powered ensuite site to a cabin at the last minute due to weather.
The apartment was lovely however a couple of detractions were noted. Nothing that detracted from the experience but more in line with an older cabin that is due for a freshen up.
Excited to see what their new cabins will be like in the near future.
Water park and pool were fantastic and the kids had a blast and the whole family enjoyed the buggies!!
Great location just outside of Forster that is quiet and enjoyable right on the river.
Great for water loving families and families who want to explore the beautiful hiking trails around the barrington tops area!

We had a very nice stay! The pool, water park and available amenities are really nice. Lovely cafe on site and they ran lots of activities to keep the kids busy during the school holiday.

The new cabins are clean and nice, and well resourced, though quite small. It rained on and off for our whole stay and each time the whole balcony got wet. I wish they’d made the veranda roof a little wider so that we could at least sit outside while it was raining.

Due to all the construction it’s mud central when it does rain. There was no clothing rack in our cabin to dry clothes off on. If any rain is predicted during your stay I recommend packing some old towels to help mop up any of the dirt and water that will inevitability make its way inside your cabin.

I wish we could have stayed longer! We only stayed for a night as we were driving from Sydney to Gold Coast and needed to break up the trip with our 1yo. We saw a water park & had to stay—did not disappoint. The staff were super friendly, even kept the water going longer for her or enjoy. So many activities, excited to come back when we have more time!

Wow. What a place to stay! The setting and location by the tidal River was terrific. The park was very well maintained, and the roads were wide and easy to manoeuvre the van. The facilities were very clean.

We unhooked the van, for our planned one night stay, and discovered an oil leak in our new Ranger. While the Ranger was in for repairs, we had the opportunity to extend our stay and take in the local surrounds.

The staff were absolutely amazing. Easy to extend our stay, and the support, advice and assistance we received due to the Ranger going in for repairs, was the icing on the cake. Georgie and David, as managers, were easy to talk to and very approachable and welcoming. Georgie went above and beyond, concerned about our food basics and supplies, given the unusual nature of our extended stay, and lack of transport. We were so pleased with the offer of a lift into Forster, to pick up a hire car. We travelled with Grant, the maintenance manager, who was easy to talk to, and knowledgeable about the local area.

Overall, we were so pleased with every aspect of our stay, and would happily return.

Thank you,
John and Pete.

Absolutely amazing! Took a four day weekend and it was perfection. Staff were friendly and welcoming, food from the on-site Cafe was tasty and fresh. Stayed in a cabin on the river and spent hours on the grass on the riverbank fishing. Couldn't have asked for a better time. Will be bringing the dog next time for sure.

We had a powered site .So can't comment on a room .
However what a fantastic family holiday park .
So much fun! I loved that we could take our dogs and that there was an off leash area. The staff were great, the cafe was fab, the location was amazing! We'll be back!!!!
Beautiful clean cabin, very polite and helpful staff, great amenities for the kids, dog friendly

Such an amazing park, staff were so friendly and attentive, room was super clean, best I have stayed I'm and well appointed.
The bed and pillows were so comfortable I need to buy one for home, great park for kids, lots of
activities

Perfect position on the river with a great cafe on site.

10/10 for Discovery Forster
Great park at a great rate
This place is gorgeous!!@! 100% recommend. Cabins are so clean!!! Would come back every weekend if I could.

It's a large park. It's currently under construction & the main entrance is confusing. It has a very small sign pointing with an arrow to reception we didn't see it & ended up driving in & around the new construction not good. Once we finally found it we were told our site number, it was so tight to get into as a tree was right on the corner of the concrete slab to park on, luckily I have a very capable Hubby who could park a van just about anywhere. It was cheap for 2 nights but unfortunately the grounds were very wet/boggy due weather. Amenities were clean

ABSOLOUTLY LOVE THIS PLACE!!!
Thanks for an amazing stay. We even added another couple of nights 🌙
Fanatstic friendly staff. And very clean park. Awesome coffee shop on site aswell. Will definatly come back!!

Out of town. Under construction. You need to drive to any shops.
Cafe on site only for breakfast.

Great park,
I'm staying here due to working in the area, & a really great location on the bend in the river. I'm in a river view cabin, very neat & tidy and comfortable.
I've got about 2 weeks here, I'm going to enjoy sitting on the deck and watching the river flow by 👍👍😊

Loved the facilities! Great staff, very accommodating. Always active, responding to my enquiries and updating me along the way.

Stayed on the new villas close to the lake. We were very impressed! the rooms were very clean & tidy. Comfy sheets, pillow and bed (none of these cheap hard beds and really soft non existent pillows from other holiday parks) The villa had everything we needed from laundry detergents for the washing machine, dishwashing liquid (do need to change to a different brand because it doesn't even bubble), they even had tablets for the dishwashing machine. You can also use their board games from the reception, dvds, bikes and scooters to for the kids.

Each room had ceiling fans and split type air-conditioning which was great! kept the whole villa warm during our cold winter stay.

If you're with Vodafone you will have zero service and wifi is close to non existent (they do have one, but it doesn't work from our villa) which is not a big downside because you get to be disconnected from all the distractions. But if you plan on working away from home during your stay this will be difficult. Unless you're with Telstra or Woolworths Mobile (they had better service than Voda).

TV wasn't smart, it was but youtube wasn't working or netflix most likely because the wifi/internet connection doesn't reach our end of the villa.

Otherwise, I can't wait to come back!! Really looking forward to summer! I would still suggest to bring your own bikes and paddles boards, they won't have enough for everyone during summer.
Nice cabin, clean, well appointed, everything you need included in the cabin b only there for one night. River lovely
Absolutely beautiful pet friendly park & location.

Lovely staff, beautiful park. Awesome fully enclosed dog park as well.
Beautiful views across water.

Thoroughly enjoyed our 4 night stay. We had a dog friendly cabin right on the river. Clean, bed comfy & kitchen well equipped. So peaceful with a large deck to watch the sun set. Had nice strolls around the park & boardwalk. Lovely pool, kids splash park & dog park. They have their own cafe on site with great coffee & meals. Dave & Georgie were lovely and very helpful with literally a last minute booking. Short drive to Tuncurry & Forster. Will definitely be back & recommending to others.
Lovely spot beside the river. Cabins showing their age. It needs to be made clear that this park is not actually at Forster but about 15kms away. Staff really helpful.
No tv reception at all powered sites ,  mud pits sites some sites have concrete , lots of mozzies when it rains, need to drive a fair way into town
Great park, popular with water skiing. Waterpark and pool are pristine and facilities kept clean
Great

Honestly don’t waste your money! Booked 12 month In advanced with 8 families for Easter - booked as a group, rang constantly through the year to keep checking we where still all together as a group and everything was okay etc - drove 5 hours and arrived and we where all spread out all over the joint, ( grandparents so far away, kids separated etc) approached reception and Georgina was the rudest person I have ever met, told they moved us to what’s left because “priority guest” booked and they put them in our cabins instead as they come for 20 years and new people don’t get to be grouped if they ring and boom and that it’s a short walk for our toddlers it doesn’t matter. Then told us “Goodluck trying to find anything Easter weekend” if we don’t like that fact they separated our group for priority guest - along with a lot of other rude comments. Nans where to far away to walk over for dinner - and driving was ro hard for them, the kids where separated Easter morning .

The facilities stunk of sewage, so unclean - our cabin was full of dog hair and the fridge kept not working, washing machine full of mould. Spending $16k for 8 families - absolutely not worth it. Rude rude rude staff, who don’t give a rats about guest. Facilities are not anything to write home about.

I could go on, I’ll be making a formal complaint to Discovery. Don’t recommend at all.

Great Park!
Massive Van sites. With huge refurbs underway!
If you don’t mind being a little out of town and your looking for river then this is for you!
Great Park for the kids with pool, splash park, play ground and pump track 👌🏼
Ants in the bathroom and Cafe so many ants ants were crawling on me in my room
Had a wonderful first time stay at this place highly recommended 😊

Even though the park is still under development and 12 kms to the north of Forster, neither of these two things are a disadvantage. The park is managed very effectively. Service staff are extremely friendly and helpful. Everything is clean and tidy. Our amenities were older but scrupulously clean and I loved the two space showers - one for washing, one for dressing. Located right on the banks of the Wallamba River, sundowners on the sandy beach or the grassy slope while the fish jump and the river changes tone, is a must. A good sized swimming pool, with river boating access and floating items for hire. An excellent camp kitchen and tidy grounds makes this a perfect spot already, let alone when all the development is finished. Bonus upon bonus, is an excellent cafe with good variety in the menu and the best coffee on this part of the NSW coast.

Splash park and pool are awesome!!!
Cabin we had was so filthy! Jumping pillow out of use and we weren’t told when booking. Food under lounges upon arrival, hair throughout bathroom, floor was filthy (my kids are very young ended up with black legs and feet from crawling) fans dirty as, exhaust fan was rusty and dusty as, crumbs throughout cutlery draw, shower with soap scum and mould !!
The location is a bit annoying if you’re going to town everyday but otherwise with kids such good facilities for them to use!!

Fantastic place 😀 first time there and it won't be the last
We loved this park

Stayed for 4 days in superior cabin. Very family friendly.  Travelled with a toddler and he loved it! So many free activities included like renovated water park, pool, bike / scooter ramp track and engaging playgrounds.

Our cabin had a great view. Close enough to the amenities but far enough from the main attractions. Peaceful.

The cafe had surprisingly good menu and healthy options. The chef was friendly and same with staff.

Customer service was lovely too. Specially PENNI from reception. Followed up on requests promptly and made us feel not like another cabin number.
So many "free" hires- boats, buggies, DVD's. Worth all the money for the services and amenities.

The river was so clear , and nice swim too.
We did not feel the need to leave the park for amusements.

We had an amazing time here over Christmas. The park has some amazing facilities, the managers put on such a fantastic week for the children filled with dance parties, pancake making, fishing comps and even a visit from Santa .
My Mr 3 loved the park, both water park and bump track and our pup was well welcomed, with a great off lead area for him to let out some energy .
Thanks guys, we'll  be back.

Great little getaway for our family. Lots to do for the kids. Took the boat and had fun on the water at our doorstep. Would be even better if there was an updated games room for the kids. Will be back!

This was an amazing stay!! Our first time being away at Christmas and booked on the recommendation of our close friends - thankfully we did!
We were in our camper trailer with the site right near the bathrooms, camp kitchen and games room and can’t fault it (our own fault for putting the camper the wrong way on the slab was the only annoyance due to the rain, but we know for next time).
The facilities were SO clean (I don’t think we’ve ever camped somewhere as clean and well-stocked in terms of the amenities) and the staff did a fantastic job of replenishing and maintaining everything throughout our stay. The kids activities planned for the Christmas period were AMAZING. Such a fantastic way to break up the day and ensure the kids stayed occupied. The activities were either free or extremely minimal cost as well so kudos to the team for pulling it off. Our friends daughter won the fishing comp and that was great fun, with the wonka pancakes a highlight for our daughters. Having the cafe open for the period was a welcome surprise as well (I needed coffee!!) with the ice cream van a lovely treat of an evening for the whole family. The location itself = amazing. Beautiful river for fishing, swimming and water sports, lots of bbqs around and plenty of bins for rubbish which were emptied regularly. The splash park, pool, playground, pump track and seating for watching the kids at all spots were great and in great condition. Huge camp kitchen, the best I’ve seen so far on our travels.
All in all we can’t wait to come back and highly recommend to everyone for a great family oriented and well-kept option for a holiday.

This was the second Christmas we have spent here and we will definitely be booking in for a third. The staff are helpful and very welcoming. All the facilities and amenities are kept clean. Love the new addition of the kids playground and the pump track is awesome. It’s very relaxing to sit on one of the many sun lounges by the pool and waterpark. This year we were in a cabin which was clean, had comfy beds and all the bits and pieces in a kitchen that you would need. We had a huge balcony to sit and enjoy the amazing view of the lake. Love the kids activities that the holiday park put on. Our fave this year was the kids fishing competition which our daughter won this year thank you for the new fishing rod and the stubbie holders 😊 There’s an on-site cafe which has great views of the water and serves up amazing coffee and food. Thank you discovery parks Darawank for making our holiday an enjoyable one 😊

This is an amazing place. The location is a short drive from the heart of Forster. The site is right on the river with multiple boat ramps and little beaches right at your door step. Great for bringing the boat or jet ski, it's a short 10 minute ride on the water to Forster Lake. The cabins are very nice and the facilities are great. A nice water park for the kids, kayaks and buggies available for free. A very calm and relaxing spot.
Had a great weekend here. Staff were friendly and welcoming. Rooms were clean and modern with enough gear for cooking and plenty of room. Kids loved the water park and pool plus pedal karts.
Our 3rd time here. Lovely park friendly staff. Will be awesome when the Reno's are finished.

Location is NOT at Forster as stated in the advertising. Miles from anywhere at Darawank nsw. False advertising imo ..Park is nice ,pool etc ,good camp kitchen but when we stayed a couple of months ago, it was out of bounds for the whole weekend due to a regular  group booking for dog training. Although their  Rules are no dogs in kitchen ,these dogs are an exception apparently.
Absolutely awesome park to stay. Great value and Kid friendly with so many activities for them and next to a beautiful lake. Even had an awesome dog park. Would recommend staying here!

We had an amazing stay at Discovery Forster. Unfortunately we were having problems with our van near the park and the kind manager Dave came over to check if we were ok, we told him what had happened and he offered for us to stay on the last available camp site, he also let us use his battery to get our van down to the site! Once we had set up, Dave had sent 2 of the lovely staff members down and offered to take us to the shops as they knew we had no means of transport. There was also a happy hour on for that evening where nibbles were provided and BYO drinks, it was nice to have a chat with everyone near the pool and the sunsets here are lovely.
All amenities very clean as well and the pool area is well kept.
Very big thank you to Dave for helping us all out!

The cabin itself was great. We were situated in a great spot. The view from our verandah was of the river, the skate park, and the playground. It's great that the kids had so much to do here. The water park was awesome.|I, the big kid, got in there with the 8yo and 12yo and had a ball.|Tossed and turned first night - not to self and others - sleep at opposite end to ladder.|2 rooms in cabin - 1 had a queen bed, the other had bunks (double on bottom, single on top). Mattress for the bottom was a bit uncomfortable, and I could feel springs. I think their mattress protectors need updating.|Staff were so accommodating and friendly.|There are plenty of bins and amenities.||We did have kids up and about til all hours of the night, both nights we were there. They were all a bit suss. First night, we had one group of kids who thought one of them wanted to bang on our cabin windows, then run - we had a baby asleep, and we were just headed to bed, too. So that shot my anxiety through the roof, I was so worried about everything that night.||It was a pleasant stay, none the less.

This caravan park is good.
Its got a lot going on at the moment.
Road in is getting redone. Lots of new cabins.
Lots to do for kids, playground, bike pump track, waterpark, pool & river.
Got boat ramp.
We had a good view of the river on 1 of the only 2 sites down the bottom.

Have 3 complaints but not venting them so only 1.
Could suggest not put people with no kids on the bottom sites next to pump track.

This provided rooms for reasonably price and rooms looked clean and modern
We had 9ppl using 2 standard cabins, all had rashes lasted a week.

It's not bedbugs, we checked.
Not sure where its coming from,
What do you use to sanitising the cabin, laundaring, and so on???
Loved it

This caravan park is designed for permanents and regular visitors as it doesn't have a lot of tourist sites. The amenities are minimal in area where tourist can stay. They are currently installing heaps of cabins, but no more tourist sites.

I’ve got a friend that lives here, & they’re always raving, just how great the place is & how well their treated respected & looked after. Also every time I’ve visited, staff are very friendly & even allow me to drive in & park while visiting.
Thank you for the fantastic accommodation only 1 nights stay, wish we were their for longer. Will definitely be back 😀
Perfect place for a family holiday! We stayed in one of the deluxe cabins with our 5, 7 and 10 year old and it was fantastic. So much stuff for the kids to do.
Ideal retreat on the river!!

The management  of this park could not give a care about other people or there children  .||This wy the management  hired out the camp kitchen to a dog group from Saturday till Sunday so thay could have dogs in the kitchen and we the campers where tolled to get out as thay have hired it so no one else  could use it I made a complaint  to management  and thay agreed its wrong and should not be allowed to have dogs in the kitchen that rule apply s to every other camper  we where also tolled that David and his wife don't care as thay say the camp kitchen it not there for our convenience  and we only pay for a site and he can shut and hire it out when ever he likes what an attitude iam also talking with the owners of the discovery parks and thay feel it is unacceptable  behaviour  from management  the camp kitchen is ther for all campers to use and thay have no right to ban others  because of there don't care attitude very disappointed  and will not return will the management  is still there even the water park for children  he shuts that on Sundays so he can bring back his soccer team and have a barbecue and drinks in there but no one else can do the same again  selfish and inconsiderate  to the people paying to go there head office in  south Australia will be looking at the comments I have made and will won't a response from the inconsiderate management  of foster discovery park

Currently staying here after travelling from Victoria. What a disappointment. We paid over $2000 for 7 nights and when we arrived were told the cabin we booked was not available and was undergoing renovations. No consideration of travel or any money in return as we have been downgraded in our accommodation. Truly disappointing for our annual holiday.

Excellent park with good facilities and very quiet. Set on river with excellent kitchen, walk tracks and good size sights. Currently in process of upgrading park so bit of work being completed during the day. Staff are outstanding and nothing but accomodating. Pool and water park, climbing frames and park for kids. Lots of beautiful beaches within half hour drive. Definitely recommend

We booked a cabin overnight because we were travelling through and only needed a place to stay. We were so blown away by the place that we all wished we had booked for a couple more nights. As it is, we were glad to arrive in the afternoon so that we could make good use of the amenities. My kids loved the pool and waterpark as well as playing on the little beach by the river. We hit jackpot with cabin #6 as it was not only next to the waterpark area but it also fronted the river and we had amazing views from our very own deck, particularly at sunset.
The cabin also exceeded our expectations. It felt brand new, was spotlessly clean and tidy, the beds were very comfortable. Reversible aircon rounded up a perfect experience. Oh, and very friendly service, too. The only downside was poor wifi connection but I had great mobile reception so I didn't really mind.
Nice enough park 'but' Discovery 'Forster' is not actually located in Forster (12kms away) 🙄
Magical

We had a great stay!
Awesome if you have dogs!
We were travelling in our van, we got an ensuite site which has a warm shower and vanity. Very cheap.
Took the dogs down to the gate dog park when we needed them to run around.
Staff were friendly.
Only thing I would say is that it's not forster, it's about 20 mins.
Other wise enjoyable stay and cheap!

We stayed at a powered site and my parents stayed in a cabin directly across from us. Penni looked after us. She and the rest of all the staff members were extremely friendly, accomodating and polite. The park was well maintained and clean with workers everywhere. Anmeties were very clean and close to our camp site. The cabin is very clean and we notice as soon as someone checked out, the staff were right in there cleaning. So many things for the kids. They had karts to ride around on, a games room, play ground, water park and pool. My husband and I loved the afternoon walk on around the river. I would highly recommend this place!! If only I doesn't get booked out so quickly during long weekends or holidays. I am definitely going to make this our regular camping place
Nice location and clean room

Omg where do.i start.

From. The Moment I got there I was in heaven.... the family was amazed and the welcome we got was above and beyond .

We already re booked a other stay and will be doing more booking for many years to come
Great location, very peaceful and quiet. On the riverfront, it's a great spot for fishing and kayaking.

Do your research. This is nowhere near Forster. Travelling in a bus we needed to taxi to and from foster @ $50 each way. Staff while we were at the park (24/6 - 28/6) were absolutely fantastic. Unfortunately we then had the displeasure of a phone call from a lady after we had left that was not so lovely. We stayed an extra day and on departure the gentleman we spoke to said they would just charge to our card they had on record which was great. Lady that called claimed they didn’t have such record even throw an email from them had been sent one hour before phone call with our card details in the email.
Apart from this staff member and being a long way from Forster it is a nice place.
A very picturesque clean & tidy park. Amenities super clean & in excellent condition. A beautiful river/lake backdrop. A real 'find'.

We had an amazing time at Forster Discovery park. We were assisted by Penny who's been so wonderful, friendly and accomodating to my call during booking. We had an upgraded cabin and we loved it! Kids didn't even want to go home. Everything we needed was there from kids entertainment, water park, playground, amazing view, comfy beds and newly renovated cabin etc. There's a lot to love in this place. We also met the lovely staff! Great costumer service! One of them let us  use the trikes early Sunday morning. She was welcoming and accomodating too and made the kids happy for  the morning ride! We'll definitely be back soon!😊
